atoms.content: KNN classifies a new data point by looking at the 'K' closest data points in the training set and having them vote on the new point's category based on distance.
If you move to a new neighborhood, you're likely similar to your closest neighbors. If 4 of your 5 closest neighbors vote Democrat, KNN predicts you do too.
KNN is a 'lazy learner.' It doesn't actually build an internal mathematical model during training; it just memorizes the data and does all the math during prediction.

1. Distance-based (requires feature scaling!).
2. No training phase (Lazy).
3. Slow at prediction time.
Because KNN relies entirely on measuring the distance between points, what critical preprocessing step must you do to your data first? (Answer: Feature Scaling).
